title: Topological superconductivity in a Hubbard model for twisted bilayer cuprates
abstract: We investigate the emergence of nontrivial topology in a twisted cuprate bilayer described by the Hubbard model in the weak-interaction regime. Our results show that the topological character depends sensitively on the doping level. For $U/t=3.85$, the Chern number assumes a value of $\pm 8$ in the electron-doped case, whereas it vanishes (0) in the hole-doped regime. The presence of nontrivial topology is further supported by an analysis the associated edge states and their chirality in a finite-width geometry, while keeping full correlations.
summary: This paper explores how topology emerges in twisted bilayer cuprates using the Hubbard model. It demonstrates that the system's topological properties, specifically the Chern number, change drastically depending on whether the system is electron-doped or hole-doped. The findings are supported by the identification of chiral edge states.
main_problem: Investigating the emergence of nontrivial topology and Chern numbers in a twisted bilayer cuprate system.
main_result: The topological character is highly sensitive to doping, with a Chern number of +/- 8 found in the electron-doped regime and a vanishing Chern number in the hole-doped regime.
method: Numerical analysis of the Hubbard model in the weak-interaction regime, including edge state and chirality analysis in finite-width geometry.
model_or_system: A twisted cuprate bilayer described by the Hubbard model.
key_observables: Chern number, edge states, and chirality.
important_parameters: Interaction strength U/t = 3.85, doping level (electron-doped vs. hole-doped), and finite-width geometry.
main_assumptions: Weak-interaction regime (U/t = 3.85) and the use of the Hubbard model to represent the bilayer.
paper_structure: The paper investigates the Hubbard model for twisted cuprates, analyzes the dependence of the Chern number on doping, and verifies topology via edge state analysis.
